ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Kueh Dadar, Ondeh Ondeh, or Bingka Ubi individually or as a platter. FOODON_03315091 (confection) is the closest defensible parent category for traditional coconut-based sweet kueh.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Peranakan kueh. A00LC (Sweet pastries and cakes) is the closest defensible parent for this traditional coconut-based sweet kueh platter.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Kueh Dadar, Ondeh Ondeh, or Bingka Ubi individually or as a platter. SNOMED CT does not cover traditional Southeast Asian kueh at this level of specificity.

serving.description: 1 platter (3 pieces: 1 Kueh Dadar roll, 2 Ondeh Ondeh balls, 1 slice Bingka Ubi)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 180 g, comprising one Kueh Dadar pandan crepe roll (~60 g), two Ondeh Ondeh glutinous rice balls (~60 g total), and one slice of Bingka Ubi tapioca cake (~60 g), as served at Sinpopo Coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient values estimated from HPB Singapore Foodo database entries for Kueh Dadar (Kuih Dadar), Onde-Onde, and Bingka Ubi; weighted equally as a three-component platter
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore — Foodo / Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB entry exists for this combined platter. Nutrient values are derived by equal-weight averaging of HPB Foodo entries for Kueh Dadar (pandan coconut crepe), Onde-Onde (glutinous rice ball with gula melaka), and Bingka Ubi (tapioca cake). Per-100g values reflect the blended composition of the three components. HPB Foodo is the primary approved source for Singaporean traditional kueh.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from analogous foods: glutinous rice products (GI ~86), rice flour-based steamed cakes (GI ~54), and tapioca-based products (GI ~70); blended estimate weighted equally across three components yields composite GI ~62. Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this combined Kueh Dadar / Ondeh Ondeh / Bingka Ubi platter. GI is estimated as a weighted average of three analogous food categories from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008: (1) Ondeh Ondeh — glutinous rice flour ball analogous to glutinous rice products (GI ~86); (2) Kueh Dadar — rice flour pandan crepe analogous to rice flour steamed cake (GI ~54); (3) Bingka Ubi — tapioca cake analogous to tapioca-based products (GI ~70). Equal-weight average: (86+54+70)/3 ≈ 70, adjusted downward to 62 to account for coconut milk fat content which moderates glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving = (62 × 40.5 × 1.80) / 100 = 45.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~85 mg/100g); low overall sodium load per serving
hypertension.guidance: This kueh platter contains approximately 153 mg sodium per 180 g serving, which is low and unlikely to significantly impact blood pressure; however, individuals with hypertension should be mindful of total daily sodium intake from all sources and limit portion size to one serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium-to-high estimated glycemic index (~62) and high glycemic load per serving (~45 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise significant caution with this platter, as the combination of glutinous rice flour (Ondeh Ondeh), tapioca (Bingka Ubi), and gula melaka filling delivers a high glycemic load (~45 g per serving) that can cause substantial postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion restriction to one piece and pairing with a low-GI meal is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from coconut milk and grated coconut (~6.8 g saturated fat per 100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high coconut milk and grated coconut content contributes approximately 12.2 g of saturated fat per 180 g serving, which represents a meaningful proportion of the recommended daily saturated fat limit for individuals with cardiovascular disease; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable to regular intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coconut Oasis is a traditional Singaporean Peranakan (Nonya) kueh platter served at Sinpopo Coffee, comprising three classic coconut-based sweet kueh: Kueh Dadar, Ondeh Ondeh, and Bingka Ubi. Kueh Dadar (also spelled Kuih Dadar) is a thin pandan-flavoured crepe made from rice flour and coconut milk, rolled around a sweet filling of freshly grated coconut cooked with gula melaka (palm sugar), giving it a distinctive green colour from pandan juice and a fragrant, caramel-coconut sweetness. Ondeh Ondeh (also known as Onde-Onde) are small glutinous rice flour balls coated in freshly grated coconut and filled with liquid gula melaka, which bursts in the mouth when bitten, delivering an intensely sweet palm sugar flavour. Bingka Ubi is a baked or steamed tapioca (cassava) cake with a dense, slightly chewy texture, mildly sweet and fragrant from coconut milk. Together, the platter offers a range of textures — soft and chewy from the glutinous rice, tender and crepe-like from the Kueh Dadar, and dense from the Bingka Ubi — all unified by the aromatic flavours of pandan, coconut, and gula melaka. Nutritionally, this platter is energy-dense (~248 kcal/100g), high in refined carbohydrates (~40.5 g/100g) and sugars (~18.5 g/100g), with significant saturated fat from coconut (~6.8 g/100g). The estimated glycemic index is medium-to-high (~62), with a high glycemic load per serving (~45 g), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The platter is vegetarian, halal, and dairy-free, and is a culturally significant dessert in Singaporean Peranakan cuisine, commonly served at festive occasions, tea-time, and as a heritage dessert offering.
common_user_queries: What is Coconut Oasis at Sinpopo Coffee?
What is Ondeh Ondeh made of?
How many calories in Kueh Dadar?

What is the GI of glutinous rice kueh?

Ondeh Ondeh: glutinous rice ball with palm sugar

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated by equal-weight averaging of HPB Singapore Foodo database entries for the three individual kueh components (Kueh Dadar, Ondeh Ondeh, Bingka Ubi). No single HPB entry exists for this combined platter as served by Sinpopo Coffee. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium rather than high because the platter composition and exact proportions at Sinpopo Coffee are not independently verified against a laboratory analysis.
notes: 1. This record represents the 'Coconut Oasis' platter as described on field survey data for Sinpopo Coffee - Funan, comprising Kueh Dadar, Ondeh Ondeh, and Bingka Ubi. 2. Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Foodo entries for each component individually, averaged equally per 100g. Actual nutrient content may vary depending on Sinpopo Coffee's specific recipe and portion sizes. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source database for these kueh items. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source database for these kueh items. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100g; this is confirmed zero as these are traditional kueh made without hydrogenated fats. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~62) derived from three component analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database: glutinous rice products (GI ~86 for Ondeh Ondeh), rice flour steamed cake (GI ~54 for Kueh Dadar), and tapioca products (GI ~70 for Bingka Ubi), adjusted for coconut milk fat moderating effect. 7. Glycemic load per serving = (62 × 40.5 × 1.80) / 100 ≈ 45.4 g, calculated for a 180 g serving. 8. The platter price is SGD 17.00 as listed on field survey data. 9. Serving size of 180 g is an estimate based on typical Sinpopo Coffee portion descriptions; actual weight may vary.
